name: Green Beans in Black Bean Sauce
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp sake or other rice wine
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the green beans into equal lengths of about 1.5 inches.
2. Blanch the green beans for 3 minutes. Set aside.
3. Mince the garlic. De-seed the jalapenos and finely dice them.
4. Mix together the minced garlic, diced jalapeno, and fermented black soybeans. Set aside.
5. In a separate bowl, mix together the rice vinegar, tamari, potato starch (or cornstarch), brown sugar, and sake. Set aside.
6. Fill a glass with water and set it handy next to the stove.
7. Put a skillet over high heat. When the pan is hot, add the garlic mixture into the pan.
8. Stir fry for one minute, adding just a little water as needed to prevent sticking.
9. Add the green beans and stir fry for 5 minutes, again adding water just as needed.
10. Add the vinegar mixture, mixing until evenly coated and sauce thickens.
11. Serve immediately.
Serves 2
Comments:
Blanching: Bring a large pot of water to a rolling boil. Dump in the green beans and boil for 3 minutes. Remove promptly and dump into ice water or run under cold water to stop the cooking.
Fermented black soybeans are available in Asian markets, usually labeled "salted black beans" and found in the preserved food section in plastic bags.
